title: Adds `--append-suffix` flag to sync command
body:
Adds the option to append the sync timestamp to the destination image tag. 

This is useful for syncing source containers which have non-immutable tags to a destination which does have immutable tags, and allows a point-in-time view of the moving source tag.

For example - source `ubuntu:bionic` would be synced to `<host>/ubuntu:bionic-1664722433` with the `--append-timestamp` flag specified.

comments.body:
Thanks!

The use case does make sense; I’m a bit worried about adding a specialized option for this. (In general, `skopeo sync` does not have a good comprehensive design of the name mapping between sources and destinations.)

E.g. other users would very likely want a human-readable date. And it seem useful to at tag all images from a single sync run with the same date, so that they can be correlated. So, at least, wouldn’t something like `--append-suffix=`_string_ work?

But even that seems like a special case where we might be able to provide a more general feature… of _some_ unknown design. Go template strings? Regexp pattern + replacement? OTOH we do have to ultimately constrain it to some language simpler than Go :)

I believe appending metadata or anything else via cli would be a good idea, but doing it at least on a per-repo basis (as well as with a cli flag globally) would bring more configurability to the yaml.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
No movement on this one in over a month and it needs a rebase.  @Phuurl thoughts on going forward?

@TomSweeneyRedHat Sorry completely forgot about this 🤦‍♂️ 
Should get chance this weekend to sort it

@mtrmac @TomSweeneyRedHat PR updated 👍 

Totally agree that `--append-suffix` is a better approach as way more versatile - if users need to append the timestamp, they can just do that with a shell call to `date` passed in as the suffix
I've re-run the Skopeo test, fingers crossed.
